gpu: RTX 5070 sm_120 driver 580 (compute used for vec-add bandwidth + kernel-launch overhead)

forward_compat_sm120_standalone_ptxas.interpretation: ptxas 12.0 does not know sm_120 (RTX 5070 native arch). This is EXPECTED: per memory `reference_gpu_fire_infra`, the silicon-fire path uses driver-JIT (cuModuleLoadDataEx loads sm_80 PTX text, driver compiles to sm_120 SASS at load time). Standalone ptxas 12.0 is sm_80/sm_90-capped. Future PTX-emit cycles should target sm_80 for forward-compat unless ptxas 12.6+ is provisioned.

step2_wmma_multitile_vs_nvcc.interpretation: hexa-emit step2 (multi-K-tile, 4-iter K-loop) is 1.67x SASS instr of nvcc reference using #pragma unroll. nvcc compresses common subexpressions (loop-invariant address calcs) that hexa's hand-emit pattern repeats per iteration. Codegen improvement opportunity: const-fold / CSE on K-loop bodies.

step3_wmma_64x64_grid_vs_nvcc.interpretation: hexa-emit step3 (16-warp 64x64 grid) is 3.0x SASS instr of nvcc reference. Largest measured codegen gap. Likely cause: hexa multi-warp emit issues per-warp address arithmetic separately; nvcc reuses warp-index computation via shared register. SIGNIFICANT codegen improvement opportunity — could reduce SASS by ~2/3 with proper CSE.

step5_tf32_gemm_vs_nvcc.interpretation: hexa-emit step5 (tf32 16x16x8) is 0.78x SASS instr of nvcc reference. nvcc tf32 reference includes __float_to_tf32 conversion loop that hexa-emit skips (tf32 input bits assumed pre-formatted by ABI). HEXA WINS on this kernel — same measured pattern as step1 (RFC 067 P6).

vec_add_bandwidth_GB_per_s.interpretation: vec-add at N=16M sustains 559-596 GB/s on RTX 5070 (~62-66% of 896 GB/s theoretical VRAM bandwidth). Memory-bound; bandwidth ceiling not codegen ceiling. unroll=2 marginally SLOWER than unroll=1 (583.6 vs 596.4) — confirms RFC 069 #207 finding that unrolling memory-bound vec-add does not help and can hurt by inflating instruction footprint. CHECKMARK §4a `vec-add bandwidth` measured PASS.

kernel_launch_overhead.interpretation: Mean per-launch overhead on RTX 5070 + CUDA 12.0 driver = 2.05 μs. This is the baseline for §5f launch-overhead amortization claim (PyTorch eager pays this per-op; cuBLAS calls cost ≥5 μs each). Direct measurement validates the structural advantage hexa-emit has over per-op-dispatch stacks. CHECKMARK §4a `kernel-launch overhead` measured PASS.

honest_scope: All measurements are SINGLE-SHAPE, SINGLE-HOST (ubu-2 RTX 5070 sm_120 driver 580).
vec-add bandwidth depends on N (memory-bound at large N, latency-bound at small N) — N=16M chosen as the canonical memory-bound regime.

Kernel-launch overhead is driver-side; 2.05 μs is a CUDA driver baseline, NOT a hexa-specific number. The advantage of hexa-emit is that the SAME 2.05 μs cost is paid ONCE per fused kernel vs ONCE per op in PyTorch eager.

nvcc PTX-diff SASS ratios are CODEGEN-DENSITY ratios, NOT perf ratios. Step3's 3.0x SASS does NOT imply 3.0x slower — register-pressure / occupancy / memory bandwidth dominate. The data identifies CODEGEN improvement opportunities, not perf claims.
All measurements are NEW (no prior measurements being re-claimed). Does NOT flip any §10 closure row.

checkboxes_flipped_this_cycle.§4a_vec_add_bandwidth: [x] hexa-emit vec-add 559-596 GB/s on RTX 5070 (62-66% theoretical)
checkboxes_flipped_this_cycle.§4a_kernel_launch_overhead: [x] 2.05 μs per empty kernel launch baseline
checkboxes_flipped_this_cycle.§7a_occupancy_estimator_data: [x] per-kernel resource usage table populated for all 11 cookbook + RFC 068/069/070 PTX
checkboxes_flipped_this_cycle.§7b_cookbook_step_2_5_nvcc_sass_diff: [x] steps 2, 3, 5 nvcc reference PTX built + SASS-instr diff measured + interpretation recorded
